name.description: Human-readable name for this test file

description.description: Optional description of what this test file covers

env.description: Inline environment variables (lowest priority in resolution chain)

cookies.description: Cookie handling mode: 'auto' (default), 'off' (no cookie jar), or 'per-test' (clear the default jar between named tests; named jars are preserved). The CLI flag --cookie-jar-per-test overrides this, except when the value is 'off'.

serial_only.description: When true, this file must never run concurrently with other files under --parallel. The scheduler pins serial_only files onto a single worker that runs after every parallel-safe bucket completes. Use this for tests that share mutable state (DB fixtures, singletons).

group.description: Optional resource group name. Files sharing the same `group` land in the same parallel bucket and run sequentially within the group, while different groups run in parallel. Useful for 'all the postgres tests' or 'all the S3 tests' where an external resource forces per-resource serialization.

redaction.description: Report-time redaction policy for sensitive headers

setup.description: Steps run once before all tests. Supports include directives.

teardown.description: Steps run once after all tests, even on failure. Supports include directives.

steps.description: Flat steps (simple format). Supports include directives.

Defaults.description: Default settings applied to every request in this file

auth.description: Optional default auth helper. Explicit Authorization headers still win.

follow_redirects.description: Whether requests should follow redirects by default

retries.description: Default retry count for all steps

RedactionConfig.description: Configurable header redaction policy for JSON report output

replacement.description: Replacement string used for redacted header values

env.description: Environment variable names whose interpolated values should be redacted in reports

captures.description: Capture names whose extracted/interpolated values should be redacted in reports

TestGroup.description: A named group of test steps

steps.description: Steps in this test group. Supports include directives.

serial_only.description: When true, this test must never run concurrently with other work under --parallel. Because Tarn's parallelism unit is the file, this escalates the whole file to the serial bucket to preserve per-file isolation (setup, teardown, cookie jars, captures).

include.description: Relative path to .tarn.yaml file to include

with.description: Parameter values injected into the included file via {{ params.name }} placeholders

override.description: Deep override merged into every imported step from the included file

Step.description: A single test step: one HTTP request with optional capture and assertions

description.description: Optional human-readable description of this step. Supports multi-line YAML block scalars (`|`, `>`).

capture.description: Capture values from the response. Values can be JSONPath strings or extended capture objects with header/cookie/jsonpath/body/status/url/regex fields.

retries.description: Number of retries on failure (0 = no retries)

connect_timeout.description: Step-level connect timeout in milliseconds (overrides defaults.connect_timeout)

follow_redirects.description: Whether this step should follow redirects (overrides defaults.follow_redirects)

max_redirs.description: Maximum redirects to follow for this step (overrides defaults.max_redirs)

delay.description: Delay before executing this step (overrides defaults.delay)

script.description: Lua script to run after HTTP response for custom validation

cookies.description: Per-step cookie control. `false` disables cookies. `true` uses the default jar. A string names a specific jar for multi-user scenarios (e.g., 'admin', 'user').

if.description: Conditionally run this step only when the interpolated expression is truthy. Empty / unset / 'false' / '0' / 'null' are falsy; any other non-empty value is truthy. Mutually exclusive with 'unless'.

unless.description: Conditionally run this step only when the interpolated expression is falsy (inverse of 'if'). Mutually exclusive with 'if'.

debug.description: When true, record request/response details for this step in the report even when it passes. Equivalent to running `tarn run --verbose-responses` for just this step. Useful for keeping one hot debugging step loud without enabling verbose capture across the whole file.

ExtendedCapture.description: Extended capture specification supporting cookies, status, final URL, headers, body JSONPath, whole-body capture, and optional regex extraction

cookie.description: Capture from a response cookie by cookie name

jsonpath.description: Capture from body via JSONPath (explicit form)

body.description: Capture from the whole response body string. Use `true` to enable this source.

status.description: Capture from the HTTP response status code. Use `true` to enable this source.

url.description: Capture from the final response URL after redirects. Use `true` to enable this source.

regex.description: Regex to extract a sub-match (capture group 1 is used)

where.description: Predicate filter applied to a JSONPath array result. Keeps only elements whose fields match the { field: value } pairs here. Combine with the `first` transform to capture an object by stable identifier instead of `$[0]`.

optional.description: When true, a missing source (path no-match, header absent, regex no-match, etc.) leaves the capture unset instead of failing the step. Downstream {{ capture.X }} references where X was optional and unset produce a distinct 'declared optional and not set' error instead of the generic unresolved-template error.

default.description: Value used when the source yields no match. Implies optional: missing values never fail the step when a default is supplied. Any JSON-compatible YAML value (string, number, boolean, null, array, object) is accepted.

when.description: Only attempt this capture when the response matches the predicate. When present and unmet, the capture is skipped (variable unset, no error) the same way optional/default captures are when their source is missing.

CaptureWhen.description: Response-shape gate for a capture. Currently supports gating on status code using the same grammar as assert.status (exact value, shorthand range '2xx', or { in / gte / gt / lte / lt } spec).

status.description: Status matcher: exact code, shorthand range ('2xx'), or complex spec.

PollConfig.description: Polling configuration: re-execute until condition is met

until.description: Assertions that must pass for polling to stop

auth.description: Optional auth helper for bearer/basic flows. Explicit Authorization headers still win.

body.description: Request body sent as JSON. Can be any valid JSON value (object, array, string, number, boolean, null)

form.description: URL-encoded form body sent as application/x-www-form-urlencoded

bearer.description: Bearer token value without the 'Bearer ' prefix

variables.description: Variables to pass to the query

operation_name.description: Operation name when query contains multiple operations

MultipartBody.description: Multipart form data body for file uploads

path.description: Path to file (relative to test file)

status.description: Expected HTTP status code. Supports exact (200), shorthand ('2xx'), set ({ in: [200, 201] }), or range ({ gte: 400, lt: 500 })

headers.description: Header assertions. Values can be exact match, 'contains "substring"', or 'matches "regex"'. Header names are case-insensitive.

body.description: Body assertions using JSONPath keys. Values can be literal (exact match) or operator objects.

RedirectAssertion.description: Assertions against the final redirect target and the number of followed redirects

url.description: Expected final response URL after following redirects

BodyAssertionOperators.description: Assertion operators for body field checks. Multiple operators on the same field use AND logic.

type.description: Assert the JSON type of the value

contains.description: For strings: substring match. For arrays: element exists in array.

is_uuid.description: Assert value is a valid UUID

is_date.description: Assert value is a valid date or datetime string

is_ipv4.description: Assert value is a valid IPv4 address

is_ipv6.description: Assert value is a valid IPv6 address

empty.description: Assert value is empty (strings, arrays, objects, or null)

is_empty.description: Alias for empty; assert value is empty (strings, arrays, objects, or null)

not_empty.description: Assert value is not empty (strings, arrays, objects)

bytes.description: Assert byte length of the matched value. For `$`, uses the raw response body bytes.

sha256.description: Assert SHA-256 hex digest of the matched value. For `$`, uses the raw response body bytes.

md5.description: Assert MD5 hex digest of the matched value. For `$`, uses the raw response body bytes.

exists.description: Assert field exists (true) or does not exist (false)

length.description: Exact length of string or array

exists_where.description: Assert the target array contains at least one object matching the given { field: value } predicate. Use this instead of `$[N]` lookups and exact `length:` to assert identity-based membership on shared list endpoints.

not_exists_where.description: Assert the target array contains no object matching the given { field: value } predicate.

contains_object.description: Alias for exists_where — assert the target array contains an object with the given fields.
